buildings.ts:335 (`buyStorage()`, the U1 sibling) has the identical bare-`canAffordBuilding(B)` gate feeding a `safeBuyBuilding(B)` that forces its own buyAmt=1. It is called from legacy/AutoTrimps2.js:215 and :218, outside `buyBuildings()`'s `game.global.buyAmt = 1` / `postBuy2` window, so U1 auto-storage (Barn/Shed/Forge) also stalls for any player on the 10/25/100 buy-amount selector. Fix both sites the same way (pass forceAmt=1 as the 6th arg).

DANGLING ELSE kills the "Buy Storage" option entirely — legacy/AutoTrimps2.js:211-218. The `}` on line 217 closes the `if (!usingRealTimeOffline) {` block opened on 211, so line 218 `else if (getPageSetting('BuyBuildingsNew') == 3) buyStorage();` is the ELSE OF `!usingRealTimeOffline`, not a continuation of the BuyBuildingsNew chain (that chain already terminated at line 216 with `else if (... == 2) buyBuildings();`). Consequence: a U1 player who picks option 3 ("Buy Storage", the 4th multitoggle face at settings-defs.ts:239) gets buyStorage() called ONLY while `usingRealTimeOffline` is true — i.e. never during normal play. Option 3 is a silent no-op: no buildings AND no storage. Same class as the #64/#65 dead-option bugs. (Present identically in tests/fixtures/oracle/autotrimps.oracle.user.js:214-217, so it is longstanding, not a regression from the #66 guard.)

DEAD FEATURE (the real bug behind this one): the entire Settings-Profile GUI (save/load/switch/delete named AT settings profiles) never renders. src/modules/settings-visibility.ts:1014 `export function settingsProfileMakeGUI() { }` (an empty stub, inherited from legacy SettingsGUI.js:2045) shadows the real builder in src/modules/import-export.ts:8 because src/legacy-bridge.ts:57 spreads `...settingsVisibility` after `...importExport`. settings-defs.ts:964 calls the global — i.e. the stub — at the end of initializeAllSettings, and import-export.ts:916's own self-execute runs before the 'Import Export' tab exists and bails at line 37-38. Verified in jsdom against the real dist bundle: `document.getElementById('settingsProfiles')` === null after a full bootSettingsUI(); the global resolves to `function settingsProfileMakeGUI3() {}`. Fix = delete the settings-visibility stub (or re-order the bridge) so initializeAllSettings builds the dropdown AFTER the tab exists — and fix the index-0/1/2 splice guard at import-export.ts:144 in the same change, since reviving the GUI makes that finding live.

Same string-'true'-default defect on `AutoGigas` (src/modules/settings-defs.ts:262, `'boolean', 'true'`), with a SPLIT outcome that makes it a distinct, real bug: its functional gate at src/modules/upgrades.ts:170 is a bare truthy test (`if (getPageSetting("AutoGigas") && ...)`) so the feature itself works ('true' is truthy — checked), but the VISIBILITY gates at src/modules/settings-visibility.ts:281-282 use `== true`, which is false. Consequence: on a fresh install in U1 the "Custom Target Zone" and "Custom Delta Factor" inputs — the two settings that exist solely to configure Auto Gigas — are hidden (`turnOff`) even though Auto Gigas is ON and running, and the only way to reveal them is to click Auto Gigas off then on. Severity MEDIUM (config surface unreachable, feature still runs). Worth a grep sweep of every `createSetting(..., 'boolean', 'true'|'false', ...)` in settings-defs.ts — the string default is only latent where every reader is truthy-testing, and any single `== true` reader turns it live.

settings-defs.ts:260 `createSetting('AutoGigas', 'Auto Gigas', ..., 'boolean', 'true', null, 'Buildings')` carries the same string-`'true'` boolean default. I did not chase every AutoGigas reader to see whether any of them gate on `== true` (a truthy `if (getPageSetting('AutoGigas'))` read would be harmless), so this is a LEAD, not a confirmed finding — worth a dedicated pass, along with a general sweep for `'boolean', 'true'` / `'boolean', 'false'` string defaults whose readers use `== true` / `== false`.

Same defect, second site: src/modules/scryer.ts:152 and :154 `else if (survive("XB", cp) && !oneShotPower("X", 0, true)) {setFormation( x ); return;}` / `else if (survive("X", cp) ...) {setFormation( x ); return;}`. `x` is initialized `var x = 0;` at scryer.ts:45 and only set to 5 when `game.global.uberNature == "Wind" && getEmpowerment() != "Wind"` (scryer.ts:47-51). So in the common non-Wind-uber case the scryer's smooth-transition ladder passes numeric 0 → same silent no-op: the "switch to X before killing the scry target" step does nothing and the previous formation persists into the kill. Fix both sites (pass '0' / make `x = '0'`), or harden the AT-side wrapper.
None beyond the already-reported sibling (stance.ts:397 `setFormation(stancey)` — same falsy-0 family, reported separately as another idx).

REAL, LIVE phantom of the SAME id, in a function that IS called: `RbuyArms()` at src/modules/other.ts:227 gates every armor purchase on `game.equipment.<X>.level < getPageSetting('RCapEquiparm')`. `RCapEquiparm` is a phantom (never createSetting'd) → getPageSetting returns `false` → `level < false` is `level < 0` → false for every level >= 0 → **RbuyArms never buys a single piece of armor**; it only runs preBuy()/buyAmt=10/postBuy(). It is reachable: other.ts:237 `Rarmormagic()` calls it, and Rarmormagic is invoked from legacy/AutoTrimps2.js:358 whenever `Rdarmormagic > 0` (bogged/plague/pressure daily) or `Rcarmormagic > 0` (Toxicity/Nom). Ironically the comment at other.ts:222-226 documents fixing the *other* phantom (`RBuyArmorNew`, #58) in this exact function while leaving `RCapEquiparm` — so U2 "armor magic" is still a complete no-op. Likely fix: use the real U2 armor cap `Requipcaphealth` (settings-defs.ts:356).

Not a new bug, but a lead worth recording: an entire U2 helper cluster in equipment.ts is dead — RevaluateEquipmentEfficiency (:612) has no production caller, and its exclusive helpers RequipEffect (:586), RequipCost (:592), RPrestigeValue (:605) appear only to serve it. They survive tree-shaking only because legacy-bridge wildcard-spreads every export onto globalThis. Separately, the comment at tests/other.rbuyArms.test.ts:29 ("RCapEquiparm is a real 'value' setting RbuyArms reads for the level cap") is factually wrong — RCapEquiparm is a phantom — and other.ts:227 (RbuyArms) reads it in code that IS reachable, meaning that armor-magic path's level cap compares `level < false` → always false → RbuyArms buys no armor. That reachable site is the one worth a separate finding; it is NOT the site this finding pointed at.

src/modules/ab.ts:97-112 (ABdustsimple) and :114-129 (ABdustsimplenonhid) both ignore `noUpgrade` and `dustType`. objects.js marks two items `noUpgrade: true, dustType: "shards"` (Doppelganger_Diadem ~2628, Doppelganger_Signet ~2668); the game renders them as a grey "Unupgradable" button (4325) but `autoBattle.upgrade()` (3659) has NO noUpgrade guard — it just spends the currency and does `itemObj.level++`, and level is meaningless for a noUpgrade item. So if such an item is owned+equipped (ABdustsimple) it can be selected and SHARDS are burned for nothing. Second half of the same lead: both functions gate on `autoBattle.dust >= equips[0][1]` while `upgrade()` pays in `shards` when `dustType == "shards"` (3663-3667) — a shards-priced item can win the cheapest-cost sort, pass the dust check, and then either silently no-op (shards short → the tick's upgrade is wasted, and it will re-pick the same item every tick, starving the owned dust items) or spend shards the user never budgeted.
None beyond this finding.

src/modules/heirlooms.ts:522-543 — `Rdhsworldstaffequip()`, `Rdhsmapstaffequip()`, `Rdhstributestaffequip()` (and `Rdhsequip1()`/`Rdhsequip2()` at :486-499) are DEFINED AND NEVER CALLED anywhere in src/ or legacy/ (grep confirms: only their own definitions plus the bundle-golden export maps, which prove nothing per the legacy-bridge trap). Their intended caller `Rdheirloomswap()` calls the non-daily `Rhs*equip` twins instead (heirlooms.ts:575,578,583,587,590). That the daily variants exist, that `Rdhs1/Rdhs2/Rdhsworldstaff/Rdhsmapstaff/Rdhstributestaff` are separate createSettings with Daily-specific tooltips ("Enter the name of your mapping staff for Dailies"), and that `Rdhs == 2` is explicitly labelled "DHS: Normal — use the non-daily settings" (settings-defs.ts:163) together prove DHS: On (`Rdhs == 1`) is meant to equip the daily-named heirlooms. It equips the non-daily ones. Fix = point `Rdheirloomswap` at the `Rdhs*equip` functions and read the written `Rshouldtributefarm` flag.

HIGH — same lines, worse case, affects EVERY player with no gamma-burst shield. `gammaBurstPct` is initialized at legacy/AutoTrimps2.js:139 (and re-set at src/modules/heirlooms.ts:600) as `(getHeirloomBonus("Shield","gammaBurst")/100) > 0 ? (…/100) : 1` — the fallback when there is no burst is **1, not 0**. So the calc.ts:360 guard `gammaBurstPct > 0` is unconditionally true, and with no gamma-burst shield calcOurDmg does `number *= (1 + 1)/5 = 0.4x` (0.5x with Burstier) as soon as the health-ratio guard passes. i.e. AT silently under-reports its own damage by 60% for shield-less/low-rarity-shield players (getHeirloomBonus only returns gammaMult for ShieldEquipped.rarity >= 10, main.js:6835-6836; otherwise currentBonus is 0). Fix must both (a) make the guard fire only when a burst actually exists and (b) use the additive (triggerStacks + pct + 1)/triggerStacks form.

While checking reachability I found a second, independent defect in the same block: the U2 health-map exit is DEAD CODE. maps.ts:1095 sets `shouldDoHealthMaps = true` only inside `else if (game.global.mapBonus < getPageSetting('RMaxMapBonushealth') && !RenoughHealth && !RshouldDoMaps)`, and that same-tick value is passed straight to RmapRepeat, whose check at mapfunctions.ts:1830 is `if (shouldDoHealthMaps && game.global.mapBonus >= getPageSetting('RMaxMapBonushealth'))` — the two conditions are mutually exclusive within a tick, so the U2 health-map repeat-OFF can NEVER fire. The U1 sibling gets this right by using a `- 1` offset: maps.ts:665 `if (shouldDoHealthMaps && game.global.mapBonus >= getPageSetting('MaxMapBonushealth') - 1)` against maps.ts:419's `mapBonus < getPageSetting('MaxMapBonushealth')` guard. Consequence: in U2, health-driven map farming never stops via this path (it relies on the flag being re-evaluated false next tick), whereas U1 stops one bonus short as designed. Also worth noting: mapfunctions.ts:1833's inner `game.global.mapBonus < getPageSetting('RMaxMapBonuslimit')` is redundant with maps.ts:1103, so that repeat-OFF click is effectively unconditional on RdoMaxMapBonus.

Possible LOW-severity sibling in the U2 twin: maps.ts:1116 `if (!setZone[x].on) continue;` is stricter than the game's main.js:13412 `currentSetting[x].on !== false`. The stock/default rows carry no `on` field at all (config.js:1421-1423: `setZone: [{world: 200}]`, `setZoneU2: [{world: 10}]`), so `on` is `undefined` and `!undefined` is true -> AT's U2 block skips the default row entirely, while the game honors it (`undefined !== false`). Only bites a player who enables MAZ without ever saving a row through the UI (UI-saved rows always set `on`), hence LOW.

Dead code / possible missing feature, buildings.ts:388 and :424 — `RbuyFoodEfficientHousing()` and `RbuyGemEfficientHousing()` (and equipment.ts:807 `Requipcalc()`) have zero production callers; they are only exercised by tests (tests/buildings.characterization.test.ts:649-690), which gives false confidence that the smithylogic housing integration works. Separately, buildings.ts:638 inside `RbuyBuildings()`: `const maxHousing = (getPageSetting('RMax' + house) === -1 ? ...)` uses the for-in KEY (`house` is "0".."6"), so it reads phantom settings `RMax0`…`RMax6` instead of `RMaxHut`… — harmless only because the `housingTargets` array it builds is never read afterwards (the real loop below re-derives housing from `mostEfficientHousing()`), i.e. dead code hiding a phantom-setting read.

src/modules/other.ts:121 (and its U2 twin upgrades.ts:228) has NO `cAutoGoldenUpgrades.selected == "Void" && game.global.runningChallengeSquared` disjunct — the C2 context is simply absent from the Void→Helium fallback, even though `cAutoGoldenUpgrades` offers "Void" as an option (settings-defs.ts:814) and the mainLoop calls the fn with it (AutoTrimps2.js:281). Consequence: in a C2 with cAutoGoldenUpgrades="Void", once voids are exhausted `setting2` stays "Void" and the fallback re-calls `buyGoldenUpgrade("Void")` — the exact call that just returned falsy — so NO golden upgrade is bought at all for the rest of the C2 run (unless the ungated line-123 filler disjunct accidentally rescues it into "Battle"). Note the C2 context IS handled in the "Void + Battle" group on line 123, confirming the author knew C2 needed a disjunct here.

Same class, second instance: settings-defs.ts:273 `createSetting('RBuyBuildingsNew', 'AutoBuildings', '...', 'boolean', 'true', null, "Buildings");` is the ONLY other boolean with a string default. Its own read is fine (settings-visibility.ts:287 `radonon ? turnOn(...)`), and `grep -rn RBuyBuildingsNew src` shows NO other reader anywhere — i.e. the U2 AutoBuildings toggle is rendered but read by nobody (a #65-style dangling setting; note the U1 sibling `BuyBuildingsNew` IS read). Worth a separate check against the settings-wired net, which apparently does not cover it.

SAME BUG CLASS, WORSE BLAST RADIUS, DIFFERENT SETTING — `fullice` (settings-defs.ts:689) also defaults to the string `'false'`: `createSetting('fullice', 'Ice Calc', 'Experimental.
Always calculates your ice to be a consistent level...', 'boolean', 'false', null, 'Combat');` Its two readers in calcOurDmg are a mutually-exclusive PAIR that must cover both cases: calc.ts:273 `if (getPageSetting('fullice') == true && getEmpowerment() === "Ice") { number *= (Fluffy.isRewardActive('naturesWrath') ? 3 : 2); }` calc.ts:276 `if (getPageSetting('fullice') == false && getEmpowerment() === "Ice") { number *= (game.empowerments.Ice.getDamageModifier() + 1); }` With the stored value being the string `'false'`: `'false' == true` → false AND `'false' == false` → false (both verified with node). So on a fresh install, in an Ice-empowerment zone, NEITHER branch runs and calcOurDmg applies NO ice damage multiplier at all — the OFF path (`getDamageModifier() + 1`, the real in-game ice debuff bonus) is silently skipped, materially UNDER-estimating our damage in every Ice zone and mis-driving stance/one-shot decisions. This is arguably HIGH: unlike `addpoison`, the string here defeats the intended default behavior itself (there is no "do nothing" branch to fall into), and it reaches the primary damage function rather than just `oneShotZone`. Same one-character fix (`'false'` → `false`); worth checking `45stacks` (settings-defs.ts:690, also defaulted `'false'`) and any other `'false'`/`'true'`-string boolean defaults in the same sweep.

The string-default class is much wider than `addpoison`: `grep "'boolean', 'false'" src/modules/settings-defs.ts` returns ~30 hits (lines 219, 220, 224, 286, 296, 320, 325, 534, 535, 549, 602, 605, 611, 612, 613, 620, 626, 632, 635, 636, 637, 640, 641, 646, 653, 656, 662, 665, 688, 689, 690, 696, 697). ALL of these render as a checkmark (settings-engine.ts:34 truthiness) while behaving as off, and all swallow the user's first click (settings-engine.ts:234 `!"false"` === false). Notable second-order case: `fullice` (settings-defs.ts:689) is read at calc.ts:277 with `== true` AND at calc.ts:280 with `== false` — with the `"false"` string BOTH are false, so on a fresh install the Ice empowerment damage modifier `number *= (game.empowerments.Ice.getDamageModifier() + 1)` is silently SKIPPED in Ice zones, which is neither the ON nor the OFF design (this one IS on a live calcOurDmg path, unlike addpoison). That is a genuine damage-estimate bug worth its own finding.

Same root, but this one DOES execute: mapfunctions.ts:387-425 `RfragCheck()` reads the frag sub-toggles into a local and tests them TRUTHILY — `frag = getPageSetting('Rshipfarmfrag')` (:402, likewise Rinsanityfarmfrag :396, Ralchfarmfrag :409, Rhypofarmfrag :414), then `if (frag) cost = RminFragMap(selection, levelzones, special);` (:425). All four sub-toggles default to the string 'false' (settings-defs.ts:325/632/653/662), which is truthy. Their master (e.g. `getPageSetting('Rshipfarmon') == true`, maps.ts:1235) CAN be legitimately on while the frag sub-toggle was never clicked (visibility only gates it on the master, settings-visibility.ts:329) — so a user who enables Ship/Insanity/Alchemy/Hypothermia farming but never touches the "Frags" button gets fragment-farming behavior (cost gated on RminFragMap) as if it were ON, i.e. the OFF default is unreachable without clicking the button twice. Worth its own finding at MEDIUM.

The string-'false' boolean default is not unique to Rsmithylogic — `grep -c "'boolean', 'false'" src/modules/settings-defs.ts` = **34** settings (vs 91 correctly using boolean `false`), e.g. buynojobsc:219, cfightforever:220, novmsc2:224, fuckjobs:296, Rshipfarmon:320, ATGA2:549, Rnurtureon:602, Rmayhemon:611, Rstormon:620, Rinsanityon:626, Rexterminateon:635. All 34 render with a checkmark glyph while styled off, and all 34 require two clicks to enable on a fresh profile (settings-engine.ts:34 + :234). Their *logic* reads are safe only by luck: every one I sampled is read as `getPageSetting('X') == true` (maps.ts:1189, calc.ts:1797, buildings.ts:672, breedtimer.ts:115), which correctly fails for the string. `Rsmithylogic` is the one that inverted the comparison to `== false`, which is why it alone has a dead guard. A one-line normalization in createSetting (coerce the boolean default/loaded value) would fix the whole class; two settings also use `'boolean', 'true'` and would need the same care.

`Dailyportal` is ALSO a phantom id (portal.ts:307, inside findOutCurrentPortalLevel): `grep -rn "Dailyportal" src/` hits only portal.ts:307 and the serialized settings blobs in utils.ts — there is no `createSetting('Dailyportal', ...)`. So on a Daily with AutoPortal="Custom", `a = getPageSetting("Dailyportal") + 1` evaluates to `false + 1 === 1`, i.e. the estimated portal level is a bogus 1 instead of the user's daily portal zone. Contained impact: findOutCurrentPortalLevel's ONLY caller is checkPortalSettings (settings-visibility.ts:979), so it corrupts nothing but the same already-dead warning — but the two phantoms should be fixed together (correct ids appear to be `DailyVoidMod` and `dCustomAutoPortal`, the latter being the id that IS createSetting'd/serialized for the daily custom portal zone).

TWO leads, both distinct from finding 118.

1. HARNESS BLINDNESS (#66 class) — tests/equipment.characterization.test.ts:800-826 hand-builds an `autoTrimpSettings` object and injects the phantom ids into it:
```
  it('Wind non-daily arm drives autoLevelEquipment line-305 `challengeActive !== "Daily"` + loomswap', () => {
    ;(globalThis as any).autoTrimpSettings = {
      AutoStance: { type: 'multitoggle', value: 3 },
      ...
      loomswap: { type: 'value', value: 1 },
```
(and the `dloomswap` twin at :818-825). The test MANUFACTURES a setting that is never createSetting'd in production, then names itself as "driving" that branch. It therefore reports green coverage over a code path that can never execute in the shipped build — the exact "harness measures something the product doesn't do" failure that made #66 permanent. The settings-wired.test.ts net catches createSetting'd-but-never-read; it does not catch read-but-never-createSetting'd, and a fixture that fabricates the id defeats it. Recommend a net asserting every `getPageSetting('<literal>')` id exists in the createSetting inventory, plus forbidding test fixtures from inventing ids.

2. UI DEAD-END at src/modules/settings-visibility.ts:155-157:
```
    !radonon && getPageSetting('dloomswap') > 0 ? turnOn('dloomswaphd') : turnOff('dloomswaphd');
    !radonon && getPageSetting('dloomswap') > 0 ? turnOn('dhighdmg') : turnOff('dhighdmg');
    !radonon && getPageSetting('dloomswap') > 0 ? turnOn('dlowdmg') : turnOff('dlowdmg');
```
Same phantom `dloomswap` → `false > 0` is false → the `turnOff` arm fires EVERY tick. `dhighdmg` and `dlowdmg` ARE createSetting'd (settings-defs.ts:157-158) and ARE live-read (calc.ts:157, :894, :937), yet they are permanently hidden from the UI — the user can never configure the daily windstacking heirloom names at all. This is arguably a higher-impact instance of the same root cause than finding 118 itself (a user-facing config dead-end vs. an internal damage under-estimate). `dloomswaphd` is a third phantom (never createSetting'd), so :155 is a pure no-op.

Same phantom, WORSE and more reachable consequence — settings-visibility.ts:154-157, inside `updateCustomButtons()` which the header comment states "runs EVERY TICK (AutoTrimps2.js mainLoop)": `!radonon && getPageSetting('dloomswap') > 0 ? turnOn('dhighdmg') : turnOff('dhighdmg');` and the same for `dlowdmg` (and `dloomswaphd`, which is not even a createSetting'd id). The condition is ALWAYS false (phantom `dloomswap`), so every tick calls `turnOff` → `toggleElem` (settings-visibility.ts:20-27) sets `display:none` on both the element and its `parentNode`. Result: the `DHS: High Damage` / `DHS: Low Damage` text inputs (settings-defs.ts:157-158) are PERMANENTLY HIDDEN in the U1 (non-radon) settings GUI — a user cannot enter the daily heirloom names that `calc.ts`'s `use3daily` windstacking path (calc.ts:894, :937) and `heirlooms.ts`'s `dhighdmgshield()`/`dlowdmgshield()` (heirlooms.ts:19-20, 223-240) all depend on. Daily windstacking heirloom swapping is effectively unconfigurable via the GUI (only reachable by hand-editing the settings JSON import). Worth filing separately from idx=118.
